entry.content: This is a word puzzle for SAP PM Tables. You have to search the SAP tables and highlight them. Search the SAP PM table, copy the picture, highlight the SAP PM table in the word puzzle, and paste it in the comments. This word puzzle will help you to revise your SAP PM Tables knowledge.

entry.title: Environment, Health, and Safety in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your one-stop shop to learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.  

 SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

 

In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Release in the area of Environment, Health, and Safety. These are the topics that are covered:

 

Cross Functions for Environment Management and Workplace Safety: 

  • Planning and execution of EHS inspections (7Z7)
  • Improved operational compliance overview page with new task insight metrics (4C8)

Workplace Safety:

  • Expansion of UK-specific incident regulatory reporting (3FP)
  • Investigation overview on the investigation object page (3FP)

 

In case you do not need all the information below and briefly want to get an overview of the release highlights, please watch this video with the top four key innovations:

 

 

Video#1: SAP Cloud ERP 2602 for Environment, Health, and Safety

 

 

Planning and execution of EHS inspections



Business Background
Organizations are required to conduct inspections to comply with numerous regulations and permits, such as those from OSHA or environmental agencies. A systematic process is essential to identify, document, and correct unsafe conditions and non-conformities, thereby preventing incidents and protecting employees and the environment.

Key Features
This new capability provides a brand-new inspection management process within EHS. It allows users to centrally create and manage checklists and execute ad-hoc inspections on-site using a mobile device. The functionality is fully integrated with foundational task management for planning and tracking corrective and preventive actions that arise from inspection findings.

 

 

Video#2: Demo planning and execution of EHS inspections

 

Improved operational compliance overview page with new task insight metrics



Business Background
To maintain proactive compliance and mitigate risk, environmental managers and other stakeholders need an analytical dashboard to monitor compliance data. An at-a-glance view of task statuses is essential for tracking the fulfillment of various legal and internal obligations.

Key Features
The operational compliance overview page has been improved with new task insight metrics, providing a richer analytical experience. The update introduces new charts, including graphical overviews of overdue and due tasks by location and a list of upcoming tasks. These new visualizations offer immediate, actionable insights and help stakeholders better monitor and manage compliance-related activities.

 

 

Video#3: Demo Improved operational compliance overview page with new task insight metrics

 

Expansion of UK-specific incident regulatory reporting



Business Background
Businesses operating in the United Kingdom face complex regulatory reporting requirements for incidents, governed by regulations such as Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations (RIDDOR) and the Building Safety Act (BSA). Making this process simpler and more reliable is crucial for ensuring timely and accurate compliance.

Key Features
This innovation significantly enhances regulatory reporting for the UK by expanding on RIDDOR functionalities and introducing new capabilities for BSA. The system now automatically determines the applicability of regulations and generates the necessary forms based on the incident's location. It also supports additional regulation-specific data fields to make the reporting process more robust and streamlined.

 

 

Video#4: Demo Expansion of UK-specific incident regulatory reporting



Investigation overview on the investigation object page



Business Background
Following an incident, a thorough investigation is critical for understanding the circumstances and identifying root causes. Investigation teams require a consolidated, high-level overview to effectively track the status and progress of their work, ensuring all activities are completed efficiently.

Key Features
The new investigation object page provides an intuitive and consolidated overview for the investigation team. Key features include a visual timeline of the investigation, a clear list of open and completed activities, the status of root cause analysis, and team responsibilities. This enhancement provides greater visibility and allows for more efficient management of the entire investigation process.

 

 

Video#5: Demo Investigation overview on the investigation object page





Thanks for taking the time to read this blog.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

 

More Information

For a more in-depth exploration, please note that the EHS & Product Compliance Infodays will be held in Amsterdam from March 24th to March 25th. You can view the agenda and register here.


️Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12 to January 16, 2026, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the 2602 release of SAP Cloud ERP. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

 

Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Easy Cooking, Part II: When AI Finally Served the Full ABAP Code
entry.content:
Three years ago I published a post with a suspiciously delicious title: “Easy Cooking: SM58 LUW_extractor (Extract pdf from logs in SM58 transaction)”. This is the sequel — the second serving — where the same SM58 problem returns to the kitchen, but this time the sous-chef is AI… and it finally knows how to finish the recipe.

       Back in 2023, when I first wrote “Easy Cooking: SM58 LUW_extractor (Extract pdf from logs in SM58 transaction)”, I had a very simple plan: get the PDF out of SM58, no matter what it takes. And by “what it takes,” I obviously mean: write it in C#, because my ABAP skill level then (and now) is still proudly parked at “Hello World”. 😄

So yes — I cooked the whole thing in C# first. Not because it was the “correct” SAP-native choice, but because it was the choice that allowed me to actually finish the meal without setting the kitchen on fire. The extractor worked, the PDFs came out, everyone was happy… and my ABAP editor remained untouched and peacefully unused, like a gym membership in January.

Then came the “side quest”: Could this tool live outside of .NET?
With a bit of help from AI, I managed to port the C# logic into PHP. That part was surprisingly smooth — like swapping pasta brands and still ending up with dinner. Different syntax, same recipe, same result.

But ABAP? Oh, ABAP was the boss fight.

At the time, I tried multiple times to translate the same logic into ABAP, again with AI assistance. The result was always the same: I’d get something that looked like ABAP, smelled like ABAP… and then dumped like a dramatic opera singer the moment I pressed “Activate.” The best I could get was a collection of promising fragments — a method here, a structure there, a confident comment like “this should work” — and then I’d spend the rest of the day proving that it did not, in fact, work.

So I did what any reasonable developer would do: I left it alone and pretended it was “not a priority.” 

Fast forward ~3 years…

One day, the thought returned: What if I tried again?
Same problem. Same repository. Same C# code sitting on GitHub, looking at me like: “Remember when you said you’d rewrite me in ABAP?”

This time, I decided to make it a proper challenge. I gave AI the GitHub URL and basically said:

“Here’s the C# implementation. Please rewrite it in ABAP.”

And I expected the usual: a motivational speech, a half-solution, and a polite suggestion that I should “consider using standard SAP functionality.”

Instead… AI just went to the repo, read the C# code, and came back like a calm senior developer who had already fixed the issue while I was still explaining it.

It generated the complete ABAP program — not just snippets, not just pseudo-code — an actual, end-to-end translation of the logic. The kind of output that makes you blink twice and check if you accidentally asked a coworker instead of a chatbot.

Of course, I didn’t trust it immediately. (I’ve been hurt before.)
So I did what we all do when something looks too good to be true: I tested it like a suspicious QA engineer with free time and a personal vendetta.

And here’s the plot twist:

It worked… after a few iterations.
Not “first try, perfection, angels singing.”
More like: “Nice attempt… now let’s fix this dump, and that edge case, and the one thing that looked innocent but absolutely wasn’t.”

So yes — there were a couple of back-and-forth rounds with AI and some testing on my side. But the important part is: the core solution was real, the logic was there, and with a bit of iterative seasoning, the ABAP version became fully functional.

The PDF extraction ran cleanly, the output made sense, and the program behaved like it had been written by someone who actually enjoys ABAP (which is definitely not my “Hello World” self).

So this Part II is really the story of that moment:
the same recipe, the same ingredients — but suddenly the kitchen gained a chef.

 Figure 1. Shows how rewritten code in ABAP extracts and opens PDF from SM58 LUW's log

Summary

That’s it for Part II of this “Easy Cooking” experiment. If you’ve tried extracting PDFs from SM58 logs (or fought with LUWs in general), I’d love to hear how you approached it — and whether you’d do anything differently. Drop a comment with your thoughts, improvements, or war stories.

The ABAP version (ZSM58_LUW_EXTRACT_ABAP) is available in the GitHub repository, and the AI model used for the rewrite was ChatGPT 5.2. Feel free to test it, break it, improve it — and if you spot a better way, share it with the rest of us.

NOTE:

Bonus idea for Part III (because sequels always happen): instead of the current “save the log → upload → extract” routine, we could make it even more lazy-developer-friendly. Imagine a mode where you just paste the SM58 Transaction ID, hit execute, and the ABAP does the rest — one input field, one click, one PDF… and zero file juggling.

entry.title: Professional Services in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your 2026 updates for the Professional Services industry, here you can learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.  

In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 in the area of Professional Services. These are the topics that are covered:

  • Intercompany cost allocations reconciliation review booklet (16T, 4AN) 
  • View custom fields in the billing elements list of the Manage Project Billing app (4E9) 
  • Enablement of time and expense billing without open billing plan item (4E9) 
  • Archival of Project Billing data (4E9) 
  • Seamless access to Preliminary Billing Document apps on creation of Project Down Payment Request (4E9) 
  • Improving usability for projects managers to adjust internal projects within professional service projects (J11)
  • Project staffing view for Resource Managers (6JO)
  • Ability to find resources across resource organizations (6JO)
  • Custom content in Resource Management apps (6JO)

Here you can have a look at the three top highlights of this release for the professional services industry here:

Intercompany cost allocations reconciliation review booklet (16T, 4AN) 

Reconciling intercompany costs is a common challenge that can complicate period-end closing. To provide complete transparency, we have delivered the "Intercompany Cost Allocations Reconciliation" review booklet. This powerful report gives the delivering company a comprehensive, step-by-step view of the entire resource-related intercompany billing process. It bridges the gap between the initial cost (the CO journal entry) and the internal billing document, clearly highlighting any unbilled items. With the 2602 release, the report is further enhanced to trace the process all the way to the corresponding FI journal entry on the receiving company's books, allowing you to quickly identify and close any reconciliation gaps.

 

This was also a request raised by customers on our Customer Influence Portal, which was resolved with this delivery: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/285049

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

 

Please also have a look at Thorsten Seegers blog, he is the product manager experts of this topic.

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Next let's get to the topics we have this release in the project billing area.

Please remember that Andreas Hammerschmidt updates his detailed blog “Project Billing for Customer Projects in SAP S/4HANA Cloud” with every release, so you can find a comprehensive overview of the functionality there.

 

View custom fields in the billing elements list of the Manage Project Billing app (4E9) 

To make truly informed billing decisions, you need the full context. We've enhanced the "Manage Project Billing" app to provide just that. Imagine your business uses custom fields on sales orders to track critical data like a "Project Priority Code," "Geographic Region," or a "Contractual Milestone ID." Now, you can add these fields as columns directly in your billing worklist. This simple change has a powerful impact: you can now sort your billing elements by priority to ensure high-value items are processed first, or filter by region to manage tax-specific requirements. This brings essential data to the forefront early in the process, reducing the need to cross-reference information and helping you prevent billing errors before they happen.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Enablement of time and expense billing without open billing plan item (4E9) 

Your ability to generate cash flow shouldn't be held up by administrative hurdles. To accelerate your time-to-bill, we've introduced a major flexibility enhancement for Time and Expense or Usage-Based billing. Consider a scenario where ad-hoc consulting work is completed and time is recorded, but a specific billing plan item for that exact period doesn't exist yet. Instead of waiting for a plan to be created, billing specialists can now immediately create a Billing Document Request for this work. The system intelligently handles the backend process by automatically generating a new billing plan item with a due date set to the current date. This ad-hoc billing capability, enabled via a feature toggle, ensures that you can invoice for completed work the moment it's ready, directly improving your cash flow.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Archival of Project Billing data (4E9) 

 Maintaining a lean, high-performing system is crucial for long-term operational excellence. To support this, we've expanded our data archiving capabilities. When a sales order tied to a customer project is closed and archived, the system now intelligently archives all associated Project Billing data along with it. This ensures that the complete historical record of a project is stored together in a secure, accessible archive. For system administrators, this means a reduced data footprint and better application performance. For compliance and auditing, this provides a clear and complete trail. An auditor-role user can easily view the count of archived entries, and the application log provides full transparency, confirming exactly which project billing requests were archived as part of the process.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Seamless access to Preliminary Billing Document apps on creation of Project Down Payment Request (4E9) 

A smooth workflow is a fast workflow. We've optimized the process for creating project down payment requests to eliminate unnecessary steps and keep you moving. Previously, after submitting a down payment request, the confirmation dialog would only link to the billing document app. This created a disjointed experience, forcing you to manually navigate to other apps to continue your work. With the 2602 release, the success dialog has been enhanced. It now includes direct navigation links to both the "Create Preliminary Billing Document" and "Manage Preliminary Billing Documents - Services" apps. This means you can flow from one logical task to the next without interruption, saving clicks and maintaining your focus.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Improving usability for projects managers to adjust internal projects within professional service projects (J11)

A great user experience is paramount. We've rolled out several enhancements to make project administration more intuitive and flexible. This includes new, streamlined Fiori apps like "Manage Projects – Professional Services Projects" for creating internal projects and "Manage Work Packages" for maintaining work-package demands. We've also listened to your feedback to provide more flexibility. You can now assign a resource to a project responsibility even if their work agreement starts in the future, allowing for better forward planning. Furthermore, the Project Control app now provides a complete financial picture by displaying all related Purchase Orders and Purchase Requisitions, giving you a single source of truth for all project commitments. This flexibility even extends to how you find information, as you can now edit certain SAP-delivered search models and publish them to your catalog, tailoring the search experience to your team's needs

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at these two entries for this topic on SAP Help Portal

 

Project staffing view for Resource Managers (6JO)

Effective resource management requires a bird's-eye view of project needs. The new "Manage Request Assignments" app delivers exactly that. It provides resource managers with a consolidated, project-centric dashboard showing all resource requests and their assignment status. Instead of digging into individual requests, you can now see the entire staffing picture for a project at a weekly or monthly level. This makes it easy to spot upcoming resource shortfalls or identify overstaffed areas at a glance. From this central view, you can directly navigate to the "Staff Resource Request" or "Manage Resource Utilization" apps to make proactive adjustments, ensuring optimal staffing across your entire project portfolio.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Ability to find resources across resource organizations (6JO)

In a global or matrixed organization, talent can be anywhere. Our latest update breaks down internal silos to help you find the best person for the job, regardless of their home department. Resource Managers can now use the new "Resource Organization" filter in the "Staff Resource Requests" app. This powerful filter allows you to expand your search and view matching resources from multiple resource organizations you are authorized to access. The results will clearly display the "Resource Organization" for each candidate, empowering you to build the best possible project team by tapping into a wider, more diverse internal talent pool.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

 

Custom content in Resource Management apps (6JO)

We understand that every business has unique data needs for making staffing decisions. To accommodate this, you can now add custom sections and content to Resource Management apps using our built-in UI extensibility tools. For example, you could add a custom section to the Resource Request page to display an employee's "Security Clearance Level," "Industry Certifications," or "Language Skills." This ensures that all critical decision-making information is available in one place. This level of customization is supported by our robust framework, which includes a library of released CDS views like `I_LglCntntMCntntTypeTxtAPI01` (Document Content Type - Text), giving your developers stable, SAP-approved objects to build upon.

 

More Information

If you are interested in the latest and greatest blog posts on Professional Services, please have a look at my collection blog  and also take a look at the Community page for Professional Services .

 

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.

 

Find All of Our Assets

entry.title: Production supply to next PSA in Cloud ERP
entry.content:
In many production environments, semifinished products are used immediately as components for a subsequent production step. Instead of putting these materials away into the warehouse after production, they can be supplied directly to the next production line upon goods receipt.

With Warehouse Production Integration with Synchronous Goods Receipt (Scope Item 5HO), SAP Cloud ERP enables exactly this scenario:
Warehouse tasks are created automatically during goods receipt, ensuring a seamless and efficient material flow from production to the next production supply area (PSA).

Why Use This Scenario?

Implementing this process offers several key benefits:

  • Reduced effort for end users, as fewer manual system steps are required

  • Elimination of errors caused by manual warehouse task creation
  • Faster processing due to reduced physical movements
  • A lean and streamlined production supply process

Overall, this approach significantly improves efficiency and transparency in production logistics.

 

Relevant scope item: Warehouse Production Integration with Synchronous Goods Receipt (5HO)

 

Configuration Overview

To enable this scenario, the following configuration steps and master data settings are required:

  • Activation of synchronous goods movements in the production scheduling profile

  • Maintenance of target PSA, destination bin, and warehouse in the production version of the product
  • Setup of automatic warehouse task creation upon goods receipt using the warehouse process type determination indicator in the warehouse product
  • Configuration of SSCUI 104392 – Determine Warehouse Process Type
  • Maintenance of a control cycle for the target PSA, including the destination bin

All these elements work together to ensure that the warehouse task is created automatically and the material is routed correctly.

Configuration:

  • Activation of synchronous goods movements in production scheduling profile

     

    • Target PSA and Destination bin and warehouse maintained in the production version of the product
  • Setup of automatic warehouse task creation upon receipt using warehouse process type determination indicator in warehouse product and setup
    • SSCUI 104392 – Determine Warehouse Process Type
  • Control Cycle for target PSA with destination bin

 

Example Scenario:

1. Step: Create Production Order

  • Material: SG424
  • Production Order: 1077350
    • Make sure to select the correct Production Scheduling Profile. 
    • Goods Receipt Location must be WM-managed Storage Location

2. Step: Confirm Production Order

  • Click on "Goods Movement Overview"
  • As you can see, there are two entries for goods movement. Goods receipt posting for material SG424 with movement type 101 and goods issue posting for component RM124 with movement type 261. 
  • Scrolling to the right, you will find the corresponding information about EWM Storage Bin, Target PSA and EWM Target Bin.  
  • After posting the goods movements, in the production order you will find the material document.
  • After posting goods receipt, there is an automatic warehouse task created to next production supply area
  • As maintained in the production version, after posting goods receipt, the material will be on PR-AREA. The destination bin is maintained in the control cycle. 

In conclusion, this use case demonstrates how semifinished products can be seamlessly transferred from production directly to the next production supply area without being put away into the warehouse. By automatically creating warehouse tasks upon goods receipt, manual steps are eliminated and the risk of errors during warehouse task creation is significantly reduced.

The article showed how the interaction of the relevant configuration steps and master data—such as the production scheduling profile, production version, control cycle, and warehouse process type determination—is essential to enable this scenario successfully. When these settings are correctly maintained, the synchronous goods receipt process ensures a fast, reliable, and efficient material flow.

As a result, customers can achieve a leaner production supply process, reduce physical movements, save valuable time, and work more efficiently in real-life production environments.

Questions or feedback are always welcome and highly appreciated!

Thanks for reading this blog post. Stay tuned!

Follow the PSCC_Enablement  tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

entry.title: Asset Management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to the blog post for asset management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release! This release introduces significant innovations in maintenance management, designed to enhance productivity for maintenance experts, improve safety and compliance protocols, and provide more actionable, centralized data.

Below, you’ll find a curated selection of release highlights from our core product management team. Before diving in, I invite you to watch the release highlights video, featuring a digital version of me! 😀

 

Table of Contents

New Asset Overview SAP Fiori app (+demo) 
Permit to work: new scope item (7Z4) (+demo) 
Enhancements to the Manage Maintenance Notification app (+demo) 
Further innovations 
SAP Asset Performance Management 
More information/additional links

New Asset Overview SAP Fiori app

To address the challenge of navigating multiple screens to gather asset information, the 2602 release introduces the Asset Overview app. This SAP Fiori application provides a single, unified interface for maintenance experts to view and interact with the complete hierarchy of their enterprise assets. Access to this application requires EAM - Asset Overview (SAP_EAM_BC_AOVW_MNG_PC) business catalog, which is included in role templates such as Maintenance Planner (SAP_BR_MAINTENANCE_PLANNER), Maintenance Technician (SAP_BR_MAINTENANCE_TECHNICIAN), and Maintenance Supervisor (SAP_BR_MAINTENANCE_SUPERVISOR).

Key features and capabilities:

Unified hierarchy visualization: The app offers a comprehensive top-down and bottom-up view of your asset structure, combining functional locations and the equipment installed within them into a single, traversable tree. Users can expand individual nodes or the entire hierarchy to its lowest level with a single click. A powerful search function allows users to isolate a specific asset and display its complete branch up to the top-level parent.

The new app aims to provide asset-centric data access, focused on:

  • Flexible column layout: This feature allows a user to select an asset in the hierarchy on the left-hand panel and view its detailed information on the right-hand panel without leaving the main screen.
  • Master and transactional data: The detail view displays crucial master data, such as manufacturer and model number, alongside visual indicators for warranty status (e.g., color-coded for active, expired or not maintained). It also provides direct access to related transactional data, including open maintenance notifications and orders associated with the asset.

Integrated maintenance actions: The app is not just for viewing data; it enables immediate action. Users can create maintenance notifications directly from the asset detail page through two methods:

  • Quick create: A simple form requiring a description to generate a notification.
  • Joule, SAP’s AI Copilot: Users can leverage the integrated digital assistant, Joule, to create a notification using natural language prompts without leaving the app.

Learn more watching the demo below:

 

Permit to work: new scope item (7Z4)

The 2602 release introduces the Permit to Work functionality under scope item 7Z4. This feature provides a standardized process for issuing permits for high-risk maintenance activities, ensuring work is performed in a safe, secure, and compliant manner. It is particularly critical for industries such as manufacturing, oil and gas, mining, and utilities.

At its core, the Permit to Work process serves as a formal authorization system. It's designed to foster close collaboration between your maintenance, operations, and safety teams, ensuring everyone is aligned on the required precautions to minimize risks.

Initially, this scope item is available for Germany and the US. Availability for other countries will follow in subsequent updates, with a target for all standard countries by the 2702 release.

Prerequisite scope items include Reactive Maintenance (4HH), Proactive Maintenance (4H), Operational and Overhead Maintenance (4BT), and Maintenance Resource Scheduling (4WM).

Core components and configuration

New applications: This functionality is delivered with two new SAP Fiori apps:

  • Manage Work Permit Template (F4692): Allows for the creation of reusable templates with pre-defined information. This reduces the time required to create new permits and ensures that minimum safety and data standards are consistently met.
  • Manage Work Permits (F6579): The primary application for creating, processing, and managing the lifecycle of individual work permits. Permits can be created with reference to a maintenance order, an existing permit, or a pre-configured template.

Process overview

1. Initiation: A planner identifies a maintenance activity that requires a permit and initiates its creation, selecting a permit type such as hot work, cold work, or confined space.
2. Approval: The permit is submitted for review. In the initial 2602 release, the permit is automatically set to a "Ready for Issue" status. Flexible workflow capabilities are planned for future releases.
3. Issuance: An authorized user, such as a supervisor, issues the permit to the designated technician.
4. Execution and return: The technician completes the work and returns the permit to the issuer.
5. Closure: The issuer verifies the work site, normalizes the technical object, and formally closes the permit in the system. The system supports actions to "retain and close" or "retain and deactivate" the permit if work needs to be suspended and resumed later.

 Learn more watching the demo below by Ebin Joseph, from product management:

 

Enhancements to the Manage Maintenance Notification app

The Manage Maintenance Notification (F5777) app has been updated with several new features to improve usability and provide deeper insights.

Situation handling: The app now uses situation handling to deliver instant alerts to the maintenance team when a new notification is created or a key status is updated. For example, an alert can be triggered when a notification is automatically generated due to a condition-based maintenance threshold breach, enabling a proactive response.

Advanced filtering: The list report now includes "Asset Location" and "Sort Field" as filter criteria. This allows users to more effectively narrow down the list of notifications to the specific assets or areas for which they are responsible.

Visual charts for measurement documents: For notifications generated from a measurement document threshold breach, the header now includes a chart. This provides a clear visual representation of the measurement readings against their defined thresholds, allowing for quicker analysis.

Reference order tracking: A "Reference Order" field has been added as a filter and a column in the list report. This enables maintenance teams to easily identify and track all notifications that were created as a follow-on work request from a technician in the field.

Conversational AI: Similar to the Asset Overview app, users can now create maintenance notifications directly within this app using natural language conversations with Joule AI Copilot.

 Learn more watching the demo below by Syed Kader Abdul Wahab, from product management:

 

Further innovations

Perform Maintenance Jobs app - Long text formatting: Clarity in work instructions is critical for maintenance technicians. Long text fields for maintenance orders and jobs can now be formatted. This enhancement allows for the use of bolding, bullet points, and other formatting options to structure text, which improves the readability and organization of detailed instructions and notes.

Manage Maintenance Plans app - Deletion of sort fields: To improve data quality and efficiency, the Define Sort Fields for Maintenance Plans configuration activity now includes a delete option. You can now remove outdated or invalid customized sort field values. This ensures that only accurate and relevant sort field values are available in the Maintenance Plan application, improving accuracy in plan management.

Centralized processing of maintenance order workflows in SAP Task Center: Efficiency in approval processes is a key driver of productivity. Maintenance Order workflow attributes are now accessible in SAP Task Center, allowing for centralized processing of approvals. The following attributes are included: Order Description, Order Type, Planner Group, Planning Plant, Basic End Date, Basic Start Date, Priority, and Technical Object. This centralization helps managers and approvers process tasks from a single, unified inbox.

SAP Asset Performance Management 

SAP Asset Performance Management integrates with SAP Cloud ERP and empowers asset owners, plant managers, and reliability engineers to optimize maintenance strategies, measure and improve asset performance, and follow through on your sustainability objectives. The 2601 release highlights include bulk activation and deactivation of system elements, more flexible risk and criticality assessment outcomes, CAP-based reliability assessment applications with extensibility, configurable RCM consequence questionnaires, validity periods for assessments, time-window–based rule processing, and embedded analytics dashboards. Read the complete blog post by Kate Dowle: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2601.

If you’d like to stay up to date with the latest APM innovations, follow my colleague Kate Dowle who regularly shares updates and new blog posts on the topic.
 

More information / additional links 

Find all enablement assets and update blog post for Asset Management: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management – The Link Collection

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Also, visit the Asset Management community page. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up-to-date to our latest enablement assets: Fernanda Rodrigues | LinkedIn

 

 Find all our assets

Do you want to learn more about the main innovations in different lines of business in the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release? The release 2602 showcases more 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

Enablement

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you: 

 

 ️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series!

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Asset Management as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.

entry.title: Build a Winning IT Operating Model for Your S/4HANA Investment
entry.content:
Werde zum Value Partner: Entfessele das volle Potenzial der S/4HANA-Investition mit dem richtigen IT Operating Model!

Die digitale Transformation ist immer noch für viele Unternehmen ein zentrales strategisches Ziel – und SAP S/4HANA spielt dabei in vielen Unternehmen eine Schlüsselrolle. Ein maßgeschneidertes IT Operating Model (ITOM) ist die Voraussetzung dafür, dass die Potenziale von S/4HANA voll ausgeschöpft werden können. Dieser Beitrag zeigt, worauf es beim Aufbau eines ITOM im SAP-Kontext ankommt – und welche strategischen Entscheidungen dabei zu treffen sind.​ 

Ausgangslage 

Ein wirksames ITOM entsteht nicht im luftleeren Raum. Vielmehr hängt es entscheidend vom Reifegrad der bestehenden SAP-Landschaft und Organisation ab:​ 

  • SAP bereits umfänglich im Einsatz: Wenn SAP bereits tief in der Organisation verankert ist, existieren in der Regel klare Verantwortlichkeiten, Prozesse und ein grundlegendes Verständnis für die SAP-Anwendungen und Technologien.​ 
  • SAP als Nischenlösung: Wird SAP nur in einzelnen Bereichen z.B. in der Buchhaltung eingesetzt, sind die Erfahrungen mit SAP zu arbeiten mit etwas Geschick auch auf andere Bereiche z.B. Produktionsplanung, Logistik übertragbar. Schließlich weiß die Organisation bereits, wie SAP "tickt". 
  • SAP als Neuland: Hier muss sich erst ein breites Verständnis für die SAP-Anwendungen und Technologien entwickeln im Projektverlauf und nach dem go-live im Betrieb. In Unternehmen ohne SAP-Vorerfahrung ist zusätzlich in Change-Management, Qualifizierungsmaßnahmen und kulturelle Integration zu investieren.​ 

Je nach Ausgangssituation müssen IT-Verantwortliche ihr ITOM gezielt ausrichten – sowohl organisatorisch als auch kulturell. Der enge Schulterschluss mit den Fachbereichen ist essenziell. In allen drei Ausgangssituationen hat die S/4 HANA-Einführung eine Vielzahl von Implikationen z.B. auf die Governance,  auf die Skalierbarkeit, auf den Einsatz neuer Technologien, Arbeitsweisen und Prozesse.​ 

Kritische Erfolgsdimensionen für das ITOM​ 

Beim Design des Operating Models müssen verschiedene Schlüsselbereiche berücksichtigt werden. Viele Darstellungen beschränken sich auf die 3 Dimensionen Prozesse, Technologie und Mensch. Erfahrungsgemäß lohnt sich eine genauere Aufteilung der Schlüsselbereiche 

  • Architektur: Häufig bereits zu Projektbeginn definiert, bildet sie eine feste Rahmenbedingung – sollte aber regelmäßig auf strategische Passfähigkeit geprüft werden.​ 
  • Tooling & IT-Prozesse: Von DevOps über Monitoring bis hin zu Support-Prozessen – effiziente Toolchain und klar definierte Prozesse z.B. nach ITIL sind unerlässlich.​ 
  • Organisationsstruktur: Ob zentralisiert, dezentral oder hybrid – die Struktur muss zur operativen Realität passen.​ 
  • Prozessansatz: Funktionale oder End-to-End-Ausrichtung oder beides? Der gewählte Ansatz hat direkte Auswirkungen auf Verantwortlichkeiten und Schnittstellen.​ 
  • Way of Working: Agile, klassische oder hybride Arbeitsweisen beeinflussen das Design der Rollen und Aufgabenverteilung.​ 
  • Change Management, Ausbildung, Kompetenz: Mit Unsicherheiten und Skepsis in der Belegschaft sollte offensiv umgegangen werden. Eine fokussierte Change-Begleitung ist sehr zu empfehlen für den Gesamterfolg der Transformation. 

Nicht alle dieser Bereiche sind frei gestaltbar – viele sind durch bestehende IT-Strategien oder Unternehmensvorgaben determiniert. Daher ist es essenziell, zu Beginn des ITOM-Designs zwischen fixen Rahmenbedingungen und flexibel gestaltbaren Elementen zu unterscheiden. Mit einer S/4HANA Transformation besteht die unbedingte Möglichkeit, Strukturen zu hinterfragen und "alte Zöpfe" neu zu ordnen.​ 

Wie sich die verschiedenen Schlüsselbereiche in den Projekt- und Betriebskontext einordnen lassen und welche Aufgaben der/die ITOM-Verantwortliche währende die S/4 HANA Einführung hat, zeigt die folgende Tabelle in einer Übersicht. 

 Schlüsselbereiche für den CCOE Aufbau

Strukturierter Aufbau: Vorgehensmodell zur ITOM-Entwicklung​ 

Folgende Schritte haben sich in der Praxis bewährt:​ 

1. Benennung eines verantwortlichen ITOM Leads:  

  • Sicherstellung klarer Verantwortlichkeiten für Design und Umsetzung des Operating Models. 
  • Verankerung der Rolle auf ausreichender Hierarchieebene (z. B. direkt unterhalb der CIO-/IT-Leitung).​ 
  • Aufbau eines schlagkräftigen Kernteams mit Vertretern aus relevanten Domänen (z. B. Architektur, Betrieb, Prozesse).​ 

2. Definition von Zielbild, Prioritäten und Vision​ 

  • Erarbeitung eines klaren Zielbilds für das zukünftige ITOM: Rolle, Beitrag zur Gesamtstrategie, Abgrenzung.​ 
  • Festlegung von Leitzielen wie z. B. Skalierbarkeit, Automatisierung oder Business-Alignment.​ 
  • Identifikation und Priorisierung der größten Hebel und Handlungsfelder – z.B. auch die Etablierung neuer “Spielregeln” wie Clean Core, SAP BTP first bei Erweiterungen, durchgängige Tool Chain 

(3. Optional) Bestandsaufnahme des aktuellen TOM (As-Is)​ 

  • Erhebung vorhandener Rollen, Prozesse, Tools und Strukturen (ggf. durch Interviews, Workshops, Dokumentenanalyse).​
  • Bewertung der Reifegrade in den einzelnen ITOM-Dimensionen.​ 
  • Identifikation von Redundanzen, Lücken, Pain Points oder auch Best Practices im aktuellen Setup.​ 

4. Definition zentraler Aufgabenbereiche (Building Blocks siehe Grafik)​ 

  • Clusterung aller ITOM-Aufgabenbereiche nach strategischen, Governance- und operativen Fähigkeiten - z.B. Sourcingstrategie, Business- und IT-Zusammenarbeit, Applikations- und SAP-Basis Betrieb 
  • Orientierung an IT-Prozessen und –Funktionen sowie der Business- und IT Zusammenarbeit. 
  • Klare Abgrenzung der Aufgabenbereiche zur Vermeidung von Verantwortlichkeitslücken.​ 

Aufgabenbereiche im S/4 HANA Betrieb

5. Erste Zuordnung von Rollen zu Aufgabenbereichen (High-Level)​ 

  • Benennung von Rollen pro Aufgabenbereich – z. B. Produkt Owner, Architekt, Applikations-/Prozess-Experte.​ 
  • Berücksichtigung vorhandener Rollen in der Organisation zur Vermeidung von Doppelstrukturen.​ 
  • Differenzierung zwischen Rollen und Stellen. 

6. Detaillierung von Rollen und Verantwortlichkeiten​ 

  • Detaillierung der Aufgabenbereiche und Erstellung eines RACI-Modells zur klaren Aufgabenverteilung (Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed).​ 
  • Beschreibung der Rollenprofile inklusive benötigter Kompetenzen, KPIs und Zielbilder.​ 
  • Validierung der Rollenzuordnung mit Fachbereichen, Betriebs- und Projektorganisationen. 

7. Identifikation von Lernbedarfen und Kompetenzlücken​ 

  • Ableitung von Trainingspfaden für bestehende Mitarbeitende basierend auf neuem Rollen- und Skillbedarf.​ 
  • Aufbau von Lernformaten - z. B. learning journeys, E-Learnings, SAP Learning Hub.​ 
  • Identifikation von Bedarf an Neueinstellungen oder externem Know-how - z. B. in Architektur, DevOps. 

8. Definition der Zielorganisation und Teamstrukturen​ 

  • Entwicklung eines Organisationsdesigns inkl. Teamgrößen, Berichtslinien und Verantwortlichkeiten - z.B. was gehört in das SAP CCOE und was nicht.
  • Entscheidung über Betriebsmodelle - z. B. zentral vs. dezentral, Inhouse vs. Outsourcing. 
  • Verankerung der ITOM-Strukturen im Operating Model der Gesamt-IT.
  • Organisatorischer Umgang mit neuen Themen z.B. Künstliche Intelligenz 

Ein iterativer, faktenbasierter Ansatz stellt sicher, dass das Operating Model nicht nur auf dem Papier funktioniert, sondern im operativen Alltag tragfähig ist.​ 

​ ...

Fazit​ 

Ein zielgerichtetes Operating Model für SAP S/4HANA ist ein zentraler Erfolgsfaktor für die digitale Transformation der IT-Landschaft. Es ermöglicht nicht nur einen stabilen und skalierbaren Betrieb der SAP-Plattform, sondern schafft auch die Grundlage für Innovation und Agilität. IT-Verantwortliche sollten die Gestaltung des ITOM daher als strategische Aufgabe begreifen – mit klarer Vision, strukturierter Umsetzung und konsequenter Verankerung in der Organisation. 

 ...

Torsten Scheffler ist selbständiger Berater und hat über 200 CCOE Projekte durchgeführt. 

...

Useful links: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-architecture-blog-posts/building-high-performing-sap-it-organization-the-art-of-organizational/ba-p/13464999
